message:
feat(layout): Add TruncateMiddle for smart text truncation (bd-2qcn)

Add smart truncation functions that preserve distinguishing suffixes:
- TruncateMiddle: Removes middle chars, keeping start + end
- Allocates 1/3 to beginning, 2/3 to end (where unique info usually is)
- Uses lipgloss.Width() for ANSI-aware width calculation

Example: "destructive_command_guard_cc_16" -> "destru…guard_cc_16"

This addresses bd-2qcn: entries like "destructive_command_guard_cc_1"
and "destructive_command_guard_cc_2" are now distinguishable when
truncated, instead of both showing "destructive_command_guar..."

Also includes bead state transitions from comprehensive UI review:
- bd-3v69: Closed (WindowSizeMsg handling complete)
- bd-103a: Closed (agent status detection working)
- bd-38gl: Now in_progress (ntm init epic started)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"bd-103a","title":"Fix agent status showing 'unknown' instead of actual state","description":"## Problem
Agent status in the detail panel shows "unknown" instead of the actual agent state (working, idle, waiting_input, etc.).

## Screenshot Evidence
Detail panel shows:
```
Status
  • unknown
```

## Code Locations
- internal/status/detector.go - UnifiedDetector implementation
- internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go - StatusUpdateMsg handling (line 1192+)
- internal/tui/dashboard/panels/detail.go - Detail panel rendering

## Possible Causes
1. **Status detection timeout** - Detector may be timing out before getting status
2. **Pane parsing failure** - Unable to parse pane content for status indicators
3. **Race condition** - Status fetch completing after pane data is stale
4. **Missing status patterns** - New agent output patterns not recognized
5. **Async timing** - Status update arriving before pane data is set

## Investigation Steps
1. Enable debug logging in status detector
2. Check if pane content is being captured correctly
3. Verify status patterns match current agent output
4. Check timing of StatusUpdateMsg vs pane data updates

## Diagnostic Code
\`\`\`go
func (d *UnifiedDetector) DetectStatus(pane tmux.Pane) status.AgentStatus {
    log.Debug("detecting status", 
        "pane_id", pane.ID,
        "content_len", len(pane.Content),
        "content_preview", truncate(pane.Content, 100))
    
    status := d.detect(pane)
    log.Debug("status detected",
        "pane_id", pane.ID,
        "status", status.State,
        "confidence", status.Confidence)
    
    return status
}
\`\`\`

## Fix Pattern
\`\`\`go
// Ensure status is never "unknown" if we have pane content
func (d *UnifiedDetector) detect(pane tmux.Pane) AgentStatus {
    if len(pane.Content) == 0 {
        return AgentStatus{State: "no_content", Confidence: 0}
    }
    
    // Try all detection methods
    if s := d.detectByPrompt(pane); s.Confidence > 0.5 {
        return s
    }
    if s := d.detectByActivity(pane); s.Confidence > 0.5 {
        return s
    }
    if s := d.detectByOutput(pane); s.Confidence > 0.5 {
        return s
    }
    
    // Fallback with reason
    return AgentStatus{
        State: "unrecognized",
        Confidence: 0.1,
        Reason: "no patterns matched",
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Status shows actual agent state (working/idle/waiting/etc.)
- [ ] "unknown" only shown when genuinely unable to determine
- [ ] Status updates within 2 seconds of pane change
- [ ] Status confidence indicator shown when low confidence

## Unit Tests
1. TestStatusDetector_WorkingPattern - detects "working" from tool output
2. TestStatusDetector_IdlePattern - detects "idle" from prompt
3. TestStatusDetector_WaitingPattern - detects "waiting_input" from input prompt
4. TestStatusDetector_EmptyPane - returns "no_content" not "unknown"
5. TestStatusDetector_UnrecognizedReturnsReason - fallback includes reason

## E2E Tests
1. Spawn Claude agent - verify status shows "working" during task
2. Complete task - verify status shows "idle" at prompt
3. Ask question - verify status shows "waiting_input"
4. Rapid status changes - verify UI updates correctly

## Logging
- INFO: Log status changes with before/after
- DEBUG: Log detection method used and confidence
- WARN: Log when falling back to unknown/unrecognized
- ERROR: Log detection failures with pane content sample","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:08:53.261933227Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T08:57:51.922467707Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T08:57:51.922312655Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-103a","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:17:36.034992183Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-108u","title":"Fix duplicated keyboard navigation hints","description":"## Problem
Keyboard navigation hints ('ti navigate', '1-9 select', 'z zoom', etc.) appearing multiple times in the UI footer area.

## Screenshot Evidence
```
ti navigate  1-9 select  z zoom  r refresh  ? help  q quit
ti navigate  1-9 select  z zoom  ...
ti navigate  ...
```

## Likely Causes
1. Help component being rendered multiple times
2. Keybinding hints concatenated instead of replaced
3. Footer area not clearing between renders
4. Multiple help components active simultaneously

## Files to Investigate
- internal/tui/components/help.go - help/keybinding display
- internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go - footer assembly
- Layout code that positions the help bar

## Fix Approach
1. Ensure help component renders exactly once
2. Clear help text buffer before each render
3. Use fixed footer region that doesn't accumulate
4. Add deduplication for keybinding entries

## Verification
- Check keyboard hints area shows each hint exactly once
- Test with different panel focuses
- Verify hints update correctly when context changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:08:14.109676127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T07:36:21.346145285Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T07:36:21.346076195Z","close_reason":"Merged with bd-144k - same root cause (help bar in scrollable area)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-108u","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:17:35.992342332Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1323","title":"E2E Tests: Dashboard visual regression with VHS recordings","description":"## Overview
End-to-end visual regression tests using VHS (terminal recording) to verify the dashboard renders correctly across different scenarios.

## Prerequisites
- VHS installed: \`brew install vhs\` or \`go install github.com/charmbracelet/vhs@latest\`
- ffmpeg for video processing
- ImageMagick for screenshot comparison

## Test File Location
- tests/e2e/dashboard_visual_test.go
- testdata/vhs/*.tape (VHS tape files)
- testdata/golden/*.png (golden screenshots)

## VHS Tape Files

### 1. Basic Render Test
\`\`\`tape
# File: testdata/vhs/dashboard-basic.tape
Output testdata/vhs/dashboard-basic.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200
Set Height 800
Set Theme "Catppuccin Mocha"
Set TypingSpeed 50ms

# Start dashboard with test session
Type "ntm dashboard test-session"
Enter
Sleep 3s

# Take screenshot after initial render
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/basic-initial.png

# Verify no duplication by checking status bar area
Sleep 1s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/basic-stable.png
\`\`\`

### 2. Resize Test
\`\`\`tape
# File: testdata/vhs/dashboard-resize.tape
Output testdata/vhs/dashboard-resize.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200
Set Height 800
Set Theme "Catppuccin Mocha"

Type "ntm dashboard test-session"
Enter
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/resize-wide.png

# Narrow terminal
Set Width 800
Sleep 1s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/resize-narrow.png

# Very narrow
Set Width 600
Sleep 1s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/resize-compact.png

# Back to wide
Set Width 1600
Sleep 1s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/resize-ultrawide.png
\`\`\`

### 3. Navigation Test
\`\`\`tape
# File: testdata/vhs/dashboard-navigation.tape
Output testdata/vhs/dashboard-navigation.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200
Set Height 800

Type "ntm dashboard test-session"
Enter
Sleep 2s

# Navigate down
Type "j"
Sleep 300ms
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/nav-down1.png

Type "j"
Sleep 300ms
Type "j"
Sleep 300ms
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/nav-down3.png

# Navigate up
Type "k"
Sleep 300ms
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/nav-up.png

# Select by number
Type "5"
Sleep 500ms
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/nav-select5.png
\`\`\`

### 4. Refresh Test (No Accumulation)
\`\`\`tape
# File: testdata/vhs/dashboard-refresh.tape
Output testdata/vhs/dashboard-refresh.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200
Set Height 800

Type "ntm dashboard test-session"
Enter
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/refresh-before.png

# Manual refresh
Type "r"
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/refresh-after1.png

Type "r"
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/refresh-after2.png

Type "r"
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/refresh-after3.png
\`\`\`

## Visual Comparison Script
\`\`\`bash
#!/bin/bash
# scripts/visual-regression.sh

set -euo pipefail

TESTDATA="testdata"
GOLDEN="$TESTDATA/golden"
SCREENSHOTS="$TESTDATA/screenshots"
THRESHOLD=100  # Maximum pixel difference allowed

echo "Running VHS tapes..."
for tape in $TESTDATA/vhs/*.tape; do
    echo "  Running: $tape"
    vhs "$tape" 2>&1 | tee -a vhs.log
done

echo "Comparing screenshots..."
FAILED=0
for golden in $GOLDEN/*.png; do
    name=$(basename "$golden")
    screenshot="$SCREENSHOTS/$name"
    
    if [[ ! -f "$screenshot" ]]; then
        echo "  MISSING: $name"
        FAILED=1
        continue
    fi
    
    # Compare with ImageMagick
    diff=$(compare -metric AE "$golden" "$screenshot" /dev/null 2>&1 || true)
    
    if [[ "$diff" -gt "$THRESHOLD" ]]; then
        echo "  FAIL: $name (diff: $diff pixels)"
        # Generate diff image for debugging
        compare "$golden" "$screenshot" "$TESTDATA/diff-$name" || true
        FAILED=1
    else
        echo "  PASS: $name (diff: $diff pixels)"
    fi
done

if [[ "$FAILED" -eq 1 ]]; then
    echo ""
    echo "Visual regression tests FAILED"
    echo "Check testdata/diff-*.png for differences"
    exit 1
fi

echo ""
echo "All visual regression tests PASSED"
\`\`\`

## E2E Test Wrapper
\`\`\`go
// tests/e2e/dashboard_visual_test.go
func TestDashboard_VisualRegression(t *testing.T) {
    if os.Getenv("RUN_VISUAL_TESTS") == "" {
        t.Skip("set RUN_VISUAL_TESTS=1 to run visual regression tests")
    }
    
    // Run visual regression script
    cmd := exec.Command("bash", "scripts/visual-regression.sh")
    cmd.Dir = projectRoot()
    output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
    
    t.Log(string(output))
    
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("visual regression failed: %v", err)
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Specific Assertions

### No Status Bar Duplication
Check that screenshots contain exactly one instance of status bar text.

### Proper Layout at All Sizes
- 80x24: Compact mode, essential elements visible
- 120x40: Standard mode, all panels visible
- 200x60: Wide mode, expanded layout

### Animation Stability
Multiple screenshots at same state should be visually identical (except for animations).

## CI Integration
\`\`\`yaml
visual-tests:
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - name: Install VHS
      run: |
        brew install vhs imagemagick ffmpeg
    - name: Run visual tests
      run: |
        RUN_VISUAL_TESTS=1 go test -v ./tests/e2e/...
    - name: Upload diff images
      if: failure()
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: visual-diffs
        path: testdata/diff-*.png
\`\`\`

## Updating Golden Files
\`\`\`bash
# After intentional UI changes, update golden files:
UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 RUN_VISUAL_TESTS=1 go test -v ./tests/e2e/...

# Or manually:
cp testdata/screenshots/*.png testdata/golden/
\`\`\`

## Logging
All test runs should log:
- VHS version
- Terminal size for each tape
- Screenshot file paths
- Pixel difference for each comparison
- Full command output on failure","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:13:09.085341313Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T07:44:32.949597443Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1323","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:17:46.574761055Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1323","depends_on_id":"bd-3b3f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:16:38.386465128Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-1hev","title":"Fix Agent Mail showing 'offline' when connected","description":"## Problem
The Agent Mail status always shows "offline" in the status bar even when the Agent Mail MCP server is available and connected.

## Screenshot Evidence
Status bar shows: "Mail: offline"

## Code Locations
- internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go - AgentMailUpdateMsg handling
- internal/agentmail/client.go - Agent Mail client connection
- internal/tui/dashboard/panels/ticker.go - Ticker panel rendering mail status

## Possible Causes
1. **Connection check failing** - Health check returns false even when server is up
2. **MCP server not detected** - Agent Mail MCP tools not being discovered
3. **Async timing** - Mail status check completing before dashboard ready
4. **Configuration issue** - Agent Mail URL/path not configured correctly
5. **Error swallowed** - Connection error not surfaced to UI

## Investigation Steps
1. Check Agent Mail MCP server status directly: \`curl localhost:PORT/health\`
2. Verify MCP tool discovery in Claude Code config
3. Add logging to agentmail.IsAvailable() and agentmail.IsConnected()
4. Check for errors in AgentMailUpdateMsg handling

## Diagnostic Code
\`\`\`go
func (c *Client) CheckStatus() AgentMailStatus {
    log.Debug("checking agent mail status",
        "endpoint", c.endpoint,
        "timeout", c.timeout)
    
    available := c.ping()
    log.Debug("agent mail ping result", "available", available)
    
    if !available {
        return AgentMailStatus{
            Available: false,
            Connected: false,
            Reason: "server not responding",
        }
    }
    
    connected := c.authenticate()
    log.Debug("agent mail auth result", "connected", connected)
    
    return AgentMailStatus{
        Available: available,
        Connected: connected,
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Fix Pattern
\`\`\`go
// Render mail status with more detail
func (t *TickerPanel) renderMailStatus() string {
    if !t.mailAvailable {
        return "Mail: unavailable"
    }
    if !t.mailConnected {
        return "Mail: disconnected"
    }
    if t.mailLocks > 0 {
        return fmt.Sprintf("Mail: %d locks", t.mailLocks)
    }
    return "Mail: ready"
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shows "ready" when Agent Mail is available and connected
- [ ] Shows "disconnected" when server is up but auth fails
- [ ] Shows "unavailable" when server is down
- [ ] Shows lock count when file reservations exist
- [ ] Status updates on Agent Mail state changes

## Unit Tests
1. TestMailStatus_Available - server up shows "ready"
2. TestMailStatus_Unavailable - server down shows "unavailable"
3. TestMailStatus_Disconnected - auth failure shows "disconnected"
4. TestMailStatus_WithLocks - active locks shows count
5. TestMailStatus_Refresh - status updates on AgentMailUpdateMsg

## E2E Tests
1. Start dashboard with Agent Mail running - verify "ready"
2. Stop Agent Mail server - verify status changes to "unavailable"
3. Create file reservation - verify lock count appears
4. Invalid auth - verify "disconnected"

## Logging
- INFO: Log mail status changes
- DEBUG: Log connection check results
- DEBUG: Log lock count updates
- ERROR: Log connection failures with reason","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:09:20.163796969Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T08:50:12.134790982Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1hev","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:17:36.047093099Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

## Possible Causes
1. **Connection check failing** - Health check returns false even when server is up
2. **MCP server not detected** - Agent Mail MCP tools not being discovered
3. **Async timing** - Mail status check completing before dashboard ready
4. **Configuration issue** - Agent Mail URL/path not configured correctly
5. **Error swallowed** - Connection error not surfaced to UI

## Investigation Steps
1. Check Agent Mail MCP server status directly: \`curl localhost:PORT/health\`
2. Verify MCP tool discovery in Claude Code config
3. Add logging to agentmail.IsAvailable() and agentmail.IsConnected()
4. Check for errors in AgentMailUpdateMsg handling

## Diagnostic Code
\`\`\`go
func (c *Client) CheckStatus() AgentMailStatus {
    log.Debug("checking agent mail status",
        "endpoint", c.endpoint,
        "timeout", c.timeout)
    
    available := c.ping()
    log.Debug("agent mail ping result", "available", available)
    
    if !available {
        return AgentMailStatus{
            Available: false,
            Connected: false,
            Reason: "server not responding",
        }
    }
    
    connected := c.authenticate()
    log.Debug("agent mail auth result", "connected", connected)
    
    return AgentMailStatus{
        Available: available,
        Connected: connected,
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Fix Pattern
\`\`\`go
// Render mail status with more detail
func (t *TickerPanel) renderMailStatus() string {
    if !t.mailAvailable {
        return "Mail: unavailable"
    }
    if !t.mailConnected {
        return "Mail: disconnected"
    }
    if t.mailLocks > 0 {
        return fmt.Sprintf("Mail: %d locks", t.mailLocks)
    }
    return "Mail: ready"
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shows "ready" when Agent Mail is available and connected
- [ ] Shows "disconnected" when server is up but auth fails
- [ ] Shows "unavailable" when server is down
- [ ] Shows lock count when file reservations exist
- [ ] Status updates on Agent Mail state changes

## Unit Tests
1. TestMailStatus_Available - server up shows "ready"
2. TestMailStatus_Unavailable - server down shows "unavailable"
3. TestMailStatus_Disconnected - auth failure shows "disconnected"
4. TestMailStatus_WithLocks - active locks shows count
5. TestMailStatus_Refresh - status updates on AgentMailUpdateMsg

## E2E Tests
1. Start dashboard with Agent Mail running - verify "ready"
2. Stop Agent Mail server - verify status changes to "unavailable"
3. Create file reservation - verify lock count appears
4. Invalid auth - verify "disconnected"

## Logging
- INFO: Log mail status changes
- DEBUG: Log connection check results
- DEBUG: Log lock count updates
- ERROR: Log connection failures with reason","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:09:20.163796969Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:03:33.903880821Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:03:33.903791743Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1hev","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:17:36.047093099Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1hz0","title":"Fix stale data display and refresh synchronization","description":"## Problem
Dashboard panels display stale or conflicting data because updates arrive asynchronously and aren't coordinated.

## Symptoms from Screenshot
- Fleet count "0/17" in status bar vs "17 panes" in stats bar
- Status showing "unknown" when agent is actually working
- Mail showing "offline" when connected

## Code Analysis

### Current Refresh Architecture (dashboard.go)
\`\`\`go
// Line 1338-1380: Staggered refresh intervals
if now.Sub(m.lastPaneFetch) >= m.paneRefreshInterval && !m.fetchingSession {
    // Panes refresh independently
}
if now.Sub(m.lastContextFetch) >= m.contextRefreshInterval && !m.fetchingContext {
    // Context refreshes independently  
}
// etc. for alerts, beads, mail...
\`\`\`

### Problems
1. **Independent refresh cycles** - Each data source has its own interval
2. **No generation tracking** - Can't tell if data is from same "snapshot"
3. **Async message handling** - Updates arrive in unpredictable order
4. **Derived values not updated** - Counts calculated from stale pane data

### Race Condition Example
\`\`\`
T=0:   Pane data: 17 panes
T=10:  Status bar renders: "17 panes"
T=50:  Pane fetch starts
T=100: Status bar renders: "17 panes" (stale)
T=150: New panes: 18
T=200: Stats bar renders: "18 panes" (updated)
       Status bar still shows "17 panes" until next render
\`\`\`

## Fix: Coordinated Refresh with Generation Numbers

### Data Generation Tracking
\`\`\`go
type Model struct {
    // Add generation tracking
    dataGeneration uint64
    lastDataUpdate time.Time
    
    // Group related data
    snapshot struct {
        panes        []tmux.Pane
        statuses     map[string]status.AgentStatus
        generation   uint64
        fetchedAt    time.Time
    }
}

func (m *Model) updateSnapshot(panes []tmux.Pane, statuses map[string]status.AgentStatus) {
    m.dataGeneration++
    m.snapshot = snapshot{
        panes:      panes,
        statuses:   statuses,
        generation: m.dataGeneration,
        fetchedAt:  time.Now(),
    }
    // Notify all panels of new snapshot
    m.beadsPanel.SetGeneration(m.dataGeneration)
    m.alertsPanel.SetGeneration(m.dataGeneration)
    // etc.
}
\`\`\`

### Coordinated Refresh
\`\`\`go
func (m Model) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
    case DashboardTickMsg:
        if m.shouldRefresh() {
            // Fetch ALL data together
            return m, tea.Batch(
                m.fetchPanes(),
                m.fetchStatuses(),
                m.fetchContext(),
                m.fetchAlerts(),
            )
        }
        
    case AllDataFetchedMsg:
        // Update everything atomically
        m.updateSnapshot(msg.Panes, msg.Statuses)
        m.recalculateDerivedValues()
        return m, nil
}

func (m *Model) recalculateDerivedValues() {
    // Ensure all counts are from same snapshot
    m.claudeCount = 0
    m.codexCount = 0
    for _, pane := range m.snapshot.panes {
        if status, ok := m.snapshot.statuses[pane.ID]; ok {
            switch status.AgentType {
            case "claude": m.claudeCount++
            case "codex": m.codexCount++
            }
        }
    }
}
\`\`\`

### Panel Consistency Check
\`\`\`go
func (p *Panel) View() string {
    // Skip render if data is stale
    if p.generation < p.requiredGeneration {
        return p.renderStaleIndicator()
    }
    return p.render()
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All panels show data from same generation/snapshot
- [ ] No conflicting values between status bar and panels
- [ ] Refresh cycle updates all data atomically
- [ ] "Last updated" indicator shows freshness
- [ ] Manual refresh (r key) triggers full coordinated refresh

## Unit Tests
\`\`\`go
func TestModel_DataConsistency(t *testing.T) {
    m := NewModel(testSession)
    
    // Simulate async updates
    m, _ = m.Update(PaneUpdateMsg{Panes: makePanes(10)})
    m, _ = m.Update(StatusUpdateMsg{Statuses: makeStatuses(10, "working")})
    
    // Counts should match
    if m.claudeCount + m.codexCount + m.geminiCount != len(m.panes) {
        t.Error("counts don't sum to pane total")
    }
}

func TestModel_GenerationTracking(t *testing.T) {
    m := NewModel(testSession)
    gen1 := m.dataGeneration
    
    m, _ = m.Update(AllDataFetchedMsg{...})
    gen2 := m.dataGeneration
    
    if gen2 <= gen1 {
        t.Error("generation should increment on update")
    }
}

func TestModel_StaleDataHandling(t *testing.T) {
    m := NewModel(testSession)
    m.dataGeneration = 5
    m.beadsPanel.SetGeneration(3) // Panel has old data
    
    view := m.View()
    if !strings.Contains(view, "stale") && !strings.Contains(view, "...") {
        t.Error("stale panel should show indicator")
    }
}
\`\`\`

## E2E Tests
1. Start dashboard, spawn new pane - all counts update together
2. Kill pane - status bar and panel update atomically
3. Rapid refreshes - no temporary inconsistencies visible
4. Network delay simulation - graceful staleness indication

## Logging Requirements
\`\`\`go
log.Debug("data refresh",
    "generation", m.dataGeneration,
    "pane_count", len(m.panes),
    "status_count", len(m.agentStatuses),
    "fetch_duration", time.Since(fetchStart))

log.Info("snapshot updated",
    "generation", m.dataGeneration,
    "claude", m.claudeCount,
    "codex", m.codexCount,
    "gemini", m.geminiCount)

log.Warn("stale data detected",
    "panel", panelName,
    "panel_gen", p.generation,
    "current_gen", m.dataGeneration)
\`\`\`","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:10:40.003531017Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T08:36:34.565972368Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1hz0","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:17:41.605719498Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1lca","title":"Task: Implement buildRecoveryContext in spawn.go","description":"## Purpose
Create the core function that assembles recovery context from all available sources.

## Function Signature
func (s *SpawnCmd) buildRecoveryContext(sessionName string) (*RecoveryContext, error)

## Context Sources
1. Latest checkpoint (if exists)
2. Agent Mail inbox (recent messages)
3. CM procedural memories
4. BV in-progress beads

## RecoveryContext Struct
type RecoveryContext struct {
    Checkpoint      *Checkpoint
    LastWorkingDir  string
    GitState        GitState
    RecentMessages  []AgentMailMessage
    Memories        []MemoryRule
    ActiveBeads     []Bead
}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Function collects context from all available sources
- Graceful handling when sources unavailable
- Context includes timestamps for staleness detection
- Returns nil when no recovery context available

## Technical Notes
Use goroutines with WaitGroup for parallel queries
Set timeouts to avoid blocking spawn","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:34:12.439218243Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T05:34:12.506425003Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lca","depends_on_id":"bd-38kx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:58:24Z","created_by":"import"}]}

        'refactor': 0.75,
        'docs':     0.70,
    },
    AgentGemini: {
        'docs':     0.90,
        'analysis': 0.85,
        'feature':  0.80,
        'testing':  0.80,
    },
}

## Usage
func GetAgentScore(agentType AgentType, taskType string) float64 {
    if learned, ok := learnedScores[agentType][taskType]; ok {
        return learned  // Use learned scores if available
    }
    return agentCapabilities[agentType][taskType]
}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Default scores for all agent/task combinations
- Scores between 0.0 and 1.0
- Configurable overrides
- Integrates with learned scores (Epic #15)

## Technical Notes
Store in internal/assign/capability.go
Allow config overrides","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:43:13.131776968Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T05:43:13.156281493Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1my2","depends_on_id":"bd-9tbv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:58:24Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3822","title":"Add comprehensive TUI debug mode with state inspection","description":"## Overview
Add a comprehensive debug mode to the TUI that helps diagnose rendering issues, state problems, and performance bottlenecks.

## Activation
- Environment variable: \`NTM_TUI_DEBUG=1\`
- Command flag: \`ntm dashboard --debug\`
- Runtime toggle: Press \`ctrl+d\` in dashboard

## Debug Features

### 1. State Inspector Panel
Show current model state in a collapsible panel:
\`\`\`
┌─ Debug State ─────────────────────────────────┐
│ Terminal: 120x40 (tier: TierSplit)            │
│ Content Height: 30 (header:6, footer:4)       │
│ Panes: 17 (claude:16, codex:0, gemini:1)      │
│ Active Panel: PanelActivity (cursor: 3)       │
│ Refresh: 1.2s ago (paused: false)             │
│ Fetching: session:no context:no alerts:no    │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────┘
\`\`\`

### 2. Render Timing
Track and display render performance:
\`\`\`
┌─ Render Timing ───────────────────────────────┐
│ Last View(): 12.3ms                           │
│ Last Update(): 0.8ms                          │
│ Frames/sec: 4.2                               │
│ View() calls: 847                             │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────┘
\`\`\`

### 3. Tea Log Integration
Enable Bubble Tea's built-in logging:
\`\`\`go
if os.Getenv("NTM_TUI_DEBUG") != "" {
    f, _ := tea.LogToFile("debug.log", "debug")
    defer f.Close()
}
\`\`\`

### 4. Content Overflow Detection
Highlight when content exceeds available space:
\`\`\`go
func (m Model) View() string {
    content := m.buildView()
    
    if m.debug {
        lines := strings.Split(content, "\n")
        if len(lines) > m.height {
            // Add warning overlay
            content = m.addOverflowWarning(content, len(lines), m.height)
        }
    }
    
    return content
}
\`\`\`

### 5. ANSI Code Visualization
Option to show ANSI codes visually:
\`\`\`
Normal: [bold red]Hello[reset]
Debug:  <ESC>[1;31mHello<ESC>[0m
\`\`\`

## Implementation

### Debug Model Fields
\`\`\`go
type Model struct {
    // ... existing fields ...
    
    // Debug mode
    debug           bool
    debugPanel      *DebugPanel
    renderTimes     []time.Duration
    viewCallCount   int
    lastViewTime    time.Time
    lastUpdateTime  time.Time
}
\`\`\`

### Debug Panel Component
\`\`\`go
// internal/tui/components/debug.go
type DebugPanel struct {
    width, height int
    state         map[string]string
    timing        TimingStats
    collapsed     bool
}

func (p *DebugPanel) View() string {
    if p.collapsed {
        return "  [ctrl+d to expand debug]"
    }
    
    var b strings.Builder
    // ... render debug info ...
    return lipgloss.NewStyle().
        Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
        BorderForeground(lipgloss.Color("208")). // Orange for debug
        Render(b.String())
}
\`\`\`

### Key Bindings
\`\`\`go
case tea.KeyMsg:
    if m.debug {
        switch msg.String() {
        case "ctrl+d":
            m.debugPanel.collapsed = !m.debugPanel.collapsed
        case "ctrl+l":
            m.debugPanel.LogCurrentState()
        }
    }
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] NTM_TUI_DEBUG=1 enables debug mode
- [ ] Debug panel shows current state
- [ ] Render timing displayed
- [ ] Content overflow warnings shown
- [ ] tea.LogToFile creates debug.log
- [ ] ctrl+d toggles debug panel

## Unit Tests
1. TestDebugMode_Activation - env var enables debug
2. TestDebugPanel_StateDisplay - state rendered correctly
3. TestDebugPanel_Toggle - ctrl+d toggles visibility
4. TestDebugMode_TimingTracking - render times recorded

## Logging
Debug mode should log:
- Every WindowSizeMsg with dimensions
- Every View() call with duration
- State changes with before/after
- Panel focus changes
- Fetch operations with timing","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:12:25.773408646Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T07:46:57.536446532Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3822","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:17:31.531090602Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- Template-specific AGENTS.md generation based on project type (Go, Python, Node, Rust)
- Interactive mode with Bubble Tea wizard
- Idempotent: safe to run multiple times

 {"id":"bd-38kx","title":"EPIC: Smart Session Recovery with Full Context Continuity","description":"**IDEA #1 - HIGHEST IMPACT (95% confidence)**

## What It Does
When NTM spawns agents—whether after a crash, reboot, manual restart, or returning after hours/days—it automatically reconstructs the context each agent needs to continue exactly where it left off.

## Why This Is The Best Idea
- The #1 frustration in multi-agent workflows is context loss
- Every session restart currently feels like starting over
- This transforms restarts from painful to seamless
- Users will feel like their agents have 'memory'

## How It Works
On session spawn/respawn:
1. Check for prior state: Look for existing checkpoints, Agent Mail threads, and session history
2. Query Agent Mail: Fetch the last N messages from threads this session participated in
3. Query CM (CASS Memory): Retrieve relevant procedural memories for this project
4. Query BV: Get current in-progress beads assigned to this session
5. Synthesize recovery prompt: Construct a structured 'here's what you were doing' context injection
6. Auto-send on spawn: Each agent receives this context automatically before user's first prompt

## Flywheel Alignment: Perfect
- Integrates checkpoints (already built)
- Integrates Agent Mail (already built)
- Integrates CM (part of flywheel)
- Integrates BV (part of flywheel)
- Each use makes recovery context richer

## Technical Implementation
- Changes to spawn.go: buildRecoveryContext() function
- New config options under [recovery] section
- Graceful degradation: works with any subset of integrations available

## Dependencies
Depends on: ntm init (for proper project setup)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:21:01.027989391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T06:06:06.298439844Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-38kx","depends_on_id":"bd-38gl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:06:06.298381905Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3uj4","title":"Task: Install git hooks","description":"## Purpose
Install git hooks for beads sync and Agent Mail guard during project initialization.

## Hooks to Install
1. pre-commit: Run br sync --flush-only to export beads changes
2. post-checkout: Warn about uncommitted bead changes
3. Agent Mail guard: Block commits that conflict with file reservations

## Scope
- Create .git/hooks/ scripts if .git exists
- Make hooks executable (chmod +x)
- Support --no-hooks flag to skip
- Do not overwrite existing hooks (append or warn)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Pre-commit hook runs br sync --flush-only before commit
- Hooks are executable
- Existing hooks not overwritten
- Works with or without Agent Mail

-{"id":"bd-3v69","title":"Properly handle tea.WindowSizeMsg for responsive layout","description":"## Problem
Layout doesn't properly adapt when terminal is resized, causing overflow and misalignment.

## Code Location
- internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go:1314-1330 - WindowSizeMsg handler
- internal/tui/dashboard/layout.go - Layout calculations
- internal/tui/layout/layout.go - TierForWidth() and layout tiers

## Current Implementation
The WindowSizeMsg handler at line 1314 does:
1. Sets m.width, m.height
2. Sets m.tier based on width
3. Calculates detailWidth for renderer
4. Updates cassSearch size

## What's Missing
1. **Panel size propagation** - Individual panels don't get resized
2. **Content height recalculation** - Available content height not recalculated
3. **Component updates** - Child components (lists, viewports) not resized
4. **Layout mode switching** - Transitions between tiers may be jerky

## Fix Required
\`\`\`go
case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
    m.width = msg.Width
    m.height = msg.Height
    m.tier = layout.TierForWidth(msg.Width)
    
    // Calculate available heights
    headerHeight := 8  // banner + stats + dividers
    footerHeight := 4  // ticker + actions + help
    contentHeight := m.height - headerHeight - footerHeight
    if contentHeight < 10 {
        contentHeight = 10
    }
    m.contentHeight = contentHeight
    
    // Calculate panel widths based on tier
    listWidth, detailWidth := layout.SplitProportions(msg.Width)
    
    // Resize ALL panels
    if m.activityPanel != nil {
        m.activityPanel.SetSize(listWidth-4, contentHeight-2)
    }
    if m.beadsPanel != nil {
        m.beadsPanel.SetSize(listWidth-4, contentHeight-2)
    }
    if m.alertsPanel != nil {
        m.alertsPanel.SetSize(listWidth-4, contentHeight-2)
    }
    if m.detailPanel != nil {
        m.detailPanel.SetSize(detailWidth-4, contentHeight-2)
    }
    if m.tickerPanel != nil {
        m.tickerPanel.SetSize(m.width-4, 1)
    }
    
    // Update renderer for markdown
    m.initRenderer(detailWidth - 4)
    
    // Update CASS search modal
    m.cassSearch.SetSize(int(float64(msg.Width)*0.6), int(float64(msg.Height)*0.6))
    
    return m, tea.Batch(cmds...)
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Resizing terminal updates all panel dimensions
- [ ] Content stays within bounds at all sizes
- [ ] Smooth transitions between layout tiers
- [ ] Minimum terminal size (80x24) handled gracefully
- [ ] Very large terminals (300x100) don't break layout

## Unit Tests
1. TestWindowSizeMsg_UpdatesAllDimensions - all size fields updated
2. TestWindowSizeMsg_PanelsResized - each panel's SetSize called
3. TestWindowSizeMsg_MinimumSize - graceful handling of 80x24
4. TestWindowSizeMsg_TierTransition - tier changes correctly on width thresholds
5. TestWindowSizeMsg_ContentHeightCalculation - height math is correct

## E2E Tests
1. VHS tape: start at 80x24, resize to 200x60, resize to 100x30
2. Verify no content overflow at any size
3. Verify tier transitions are smooth

## Logging
- DEBUG: Log all dimension calculations on resize
- DEBUG: Log panel sizes after resize
- INFO: Log tier transitions","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:12:16.202239127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T08:47:06.349933596Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3v69","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:17:25.829321010Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3vid","title":"Task: Implement inbox polling in dashboard","description":"## Purpose
Periodically poll Agent Mail inboxes and display pending messages.

## Dashboard Integration
- Poll every 30 seconds for each registered agent
- Display unread count in agent status bar
- Highlight urgent messages
- Support click/key to view message details

## Status Bar Display
cc_1 | Claude/Opus | 34% ctx | ✉ 2 new (1 urgent)

## Polling Logic
func (d *Dashboard) checkAgentInboxes() {
    for paneID, agentName := range d.agentRegistry {
        inbox, _ := d.amClient.FetchInbox(ctx, FetchInboxOptions{
            ProjectKey: d.projectDir,
            AgentName:  agentName,
            Limit:      5,
            UrgentOnly: false,
        })
        
        if len(inbox) > 0 {
            d.pendingMessages[paneID] = inbox
        }
    }
}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Inbox status shown per agent
- Urgent messages highlighted
- Message preview on demand
- Minimal performance impact

## Technical Notes
Use ticker for periodic polling
Cache results to reduce API calls","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:42:46.824299222Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T05:42:46.836369643Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vid","depends_on_id":"bd-3hrk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:58:25Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-4qbs","title":"Task: Create template YAML schema","description":"## Purpose
Define structured schema for session templates that capture common workflow patterns.

## Scope

## Acceptance Criteria

- Templates can specify agent types and counts
- Pre-spawn hooks can be defined
- File reservation patterns can be declared
- Validation provides helpful error messages

- Consider JSON Schema generation for editor support
- Templates stored in .ntm/templates/ by default","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:59:43.044326817Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T05:59:43.066545288Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4qbs","depends_on_id":"bd-2b35","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:59:43.066497819Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
